commands.author: JuanHuaXu
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8506c0a16516fac3b3b5a186eaf5663d922825e5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `adaptive` markdown table mode that renders Microsoft Teams markdown tables as Adaptive Card Table attachments across final reply and proactive send paths, with schema, docs, and tests.

PR surface: Source +221, Tests +226, Docs +3. Total +450 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a Teams adaptive-mode reply containing a markdown link or emphasis around a table will split and send `ir.text`, which no longer contains the link URL or formatting markers. I did not run a live Teams repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can address the source-proven markdown/link loss without deciding the broader Teams product direction.

target.title: fix: retry safe post-tool continuation turns
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents terminal assistant turns that only promise to act, such as “Hold on, let me look that up” or “Now let me get the details,” from being accepted as complete when replay is safe.
- Covers both no-tool narration stalls and replay-safe post-tool narration stalls without hardcoding provider/plugin tool names.
- Keeps existing side-effect protections intact so mutating tools, delivery, tool errors, async work, and completed/blocker-looking replies do not get blindly retried.

Why does this matter now?

- Local-model turns can stop after intent-only narration, especially around lookup/current-info workflows, leaving users with a promise instead of the requested result.
- A provider/plugin-specific allowlist is brittle because the model-facing tool name may differ across search, memory, or third-party lookup plugins.

What is the intended outcome?

- If the assistant only says it is about to act and the attempt is replay-safe, the runner issues the existing “Act now” retry instruction.
- If the assistant already produced a result, blocker, side effect, delivery, or tool error, behavior remains unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to search providers, memory plugins, tool schemas, or model prompts outside the incomplete-turn guard.
- No attempt to classify every plugin’s read-only tool names in core.
- No config or migration changes.

What does success look like?

- Replay-safe action-promise-only turns are retried instead of ending as successful empty work.
- Casual non-task chatter and side-effectful attempts are not retried.
- The guard remains provider/plugin-name agnostic.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `isActionPromiseOnlyPattern` is narrow enough to avoid casual-chat false positives.
- Whether `promptRequiresAction` and replay metadata are sufficient guardrails for the bypass.
- Whether the tests cover the intended invariant without encoding provider/plugin-specific behavior.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

No linked issue yet.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Related local investigation: a managed Gateway turn could end with only intent narration instead of producing the requested lookup result. The final source patch is intentionally generic and does not depend on the local provider/plugin names observed during that investigation.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This is a contributor bug-fix PR based on local repro/debugging.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Assistant turns that only promise a next action can be treated as complete, causing the user-visible task to stall.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout plus local managed Gateway debugging on macOS with a local model provider. The final source-only amendment was validated with the focused runner helper and test suite, not redeployed into the managed Gateway after amendment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm exec tsx -e '<direct resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction probe>'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /repo

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  115 passed (115)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Direct source helper probe:

```text
unknown-tool: retry
no-tool: retry
side-effect: no-retry
casual: no-retry
```

Formatting/checks:

```text
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
```

- Observed result after fix: Replay-safe action-promise-only attempts return the existing planning-only retry instruction; side-effectful and casual non-task attempts return `null`.
- What was not tested: Full suite, cross-provider live runs, and a managed Gateway run with this exact final amended commit installed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The real Gateway investigation used a local setup and was sanitized; the final proof here is focused source-level behavior because the PR change is a small runner guard and we intentionally avoided posting private runtime logs.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Prior to this change, an intent-only turn such as `Hold on — let me look that up.` could satisfy terminal stop handling without producing the requested lookup result. A previous tool-name allowlist attempt also failed the invariant because provider/plugin-specific tool names are not stable.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
git diff --check
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added a replay-safe post-tool narration test using generic third-party lookup tool names.
- Added a no-tool lookup narration test for the `Hold on — let me look that up.` shape.
- Added a side-effect negative control ensuring delivery-like tool activity still does not retry.
- Existing casual non-task chat coverage also guards against over-broad lookup-promise matching.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The original guard only retried provider/model surfaces already covered by the planning-only guard and rejected most non-plan tool activity.
- A naive allowlist patch missed provider/plugin-specific tool names and risked hardcoding local plugin behavior in core.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No direct security/auth/config change. The retry guard can cause an additional model attempt only when replay metadata says the attempt had no side effects.

What is the highest-risk area?

False-positive retries for casual or already-complete assistant text.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Text must be short and match an action-promise-only shape.
- Completion/result/blocker-looking text is excluded.
- Prompt must be actionable or an explicit automation envelope.
- Attempts with side effects, delivery evidence, async work, tool errors, aborts, or timeouts are excluded.
- Regression tests include casual-chat and side-effect negative controls.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on CI and maintainer feedback. A maintainer may want broader live-provider proof if the focused source proof is not enough.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No bot or reviewer comments yet. During local review, the patch was revised to remove provider/plugin-specific safe-tool allowlists and private fixture details.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>
target.author: juanhuaxu
